name: Misiones (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the federalist red of the Liga Federal of Artigas, of which Misiones was a founding member under Andres Guacurari
the blue of the Guarani caudillo's standard and of the Parana and Uruguay rivers that border the province
purity; the centre of the Argentine flag

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — red over blue over white.
history: Adopted by provincial law 3261 on 17 November 1995. The colours recall the standard used by the Guarani caudillo Andres Guacurari ("Andresito"), commander-in-chief of the Province of Misiones from 1815 to 1819 within the Federal League of Jose Gervasio Artigas, and link Misiones to its Jesuit-Guarani heritage and to the federalist tradition.

features.symbolism: a tricolour evoking the standard used by the Guarani caudillo Andres Guacurari (Andresito) — commander-in-chief of Misiones from 1815 to 1819 in the Federal League of Artigas — and the colours used in the historic Jesuit reductions of Misiones
